faq.q: What's the best JPG quality setting for web?
faq.a: 85% is the sweet spot—almost indistinguishable from 100%, but roughly 50% smaller file size.

faq.q: How much smaller is WebP compared to JPG?
faq.a: WebP is typically 25-35% smaller than JPG at similar quality levels.
Yes, AVIF is even better than WebP, with 90%+ browser support as of 2024.

faq.q: When should I use PNG instead?
faq.a: PNG is lossless and heavier; only use it for graphics with transparency.
Progressive JPG loads in passes, improving perceived load time and user experience.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: How accurate are the file size estimates?
faq.a: These are estimates using industry-standard formulas—close but not pixel-perfect. For critical work, test in your actual environment.

faq.q: Do you store my image data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send any data to our servers.
